div.p: Healthcare industry has embraced FHIR as the standard for data exchange and has been implementing FHIR in the real-world as part of the various accelerators such as Argonaut, DaVinci, Helios. The adoption of FHIR has been further expedited by the ASTP/ONC and CMS regulations which require the implementation of FHIR for multiple use cases. One of the competing requirement that is emerging in the industry is the need for data which does not contain PHI/PII. These requirements are common among Federal Reporting use cases such as HRSA Uniform Data System (UDS) reporting, Public Health reporting to CDC, data needs for training AI models, data needs for research programs. This implementation guide creates a set of services that can deidentify, anonymize USCDI resources based on a policy identifer.

This Implementation Guide (IG) defines the specifications by which Federal Agencies such as HRSA, SAMSHA and others can receive line level de-identified information and can publish anonymized data sets. The IG specifies services to
De-identify Patient Data based on a policy identifier
Anonymize healthcare data sets using a specific techniques
The main sections of this IG are:

ul.li: Background - The page provides introduction and provides the requirements in scope for the IG.
Use Cases - Defines the use case, workflows and definitions that will be used as part of the IG.
Formal Specification - Defines the formal specification in terms of requirements that need to be implemented by a service provider.
FHIR Artifacts - Defines the FHIR Artifacts for the IG.
Downloads - Allows downloading a copy of this implementation guide and other useful information

div.p: This section elaborates the relationship of this IG to other implementation guides
div.h4: Relationship to US Core Implmentation Guide
div.p: This implementation guide, leverages terminology from US Core, and also uses US Core profiles to specify the inputs to the DARTS services. The outputs of DARTS services will be represented using profiles specified in the DAPL IG.
div.h4: Relationship to De-identified, Anonymized FHIR Profiles Library Implmentation Guide
div.p: This implementation guide complements the the De-identified, Anonymized FHIR Profiles Library (DAPL) Implmentation Guide by defining the services that implement techniques and algorithms for deidentification and anonymization, where as the DAPL IG defines the set of profiles to represent the deidentified and anonymized information.
div.h4: Relationship to Data Segmentation for Privacy (DS4P) Implmentation Guide
div.p: DS4P IG specifies the tags,labels,obligations to be used to protect patient privacy of patients' data. DARTS IG on the other hand aims to remove PHI/PII from the data and hence will not be using the DS4P tags currently.
div.h4: Relationship to HRSA UDS+ Implmentation Guide
div.p: HRSA UDS+ IG defines the specific content and APIs for Health Centers to report data to HRSA annually. UDS+ will be reusing services from DARTS IG to create deidentified content and the DAPL IG profiles to represent deidentified content.
